However, the rest of the information\n   358→        # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n   359→        #\n   360→        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n   361→        # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n   362→        # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n   363→        # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n   368→        # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n   369→        # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n   370→        # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n   371→        #\n   372→        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n   373→        # | Processes:                                                                  |\n   374→        # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n   375→        # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n   376→        # |=============================================================================|\n   377→        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n   378→        #\n   379→        # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n   380→        # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n   381→        #\n   382→        # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n   383→        # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n   384→        nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n   385→        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n   386→\n   387→        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n   388→        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n   389→          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n   390→          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n   391→        fi\n   392→\n   393→        # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n   394→        # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n   395→        # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n   396→        GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n   397→        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n   398→\n   399→        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n   400→        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n   401→          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n   402→          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n   403→        fi\n   404→\n   405→        # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n   406→        if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n   407→          echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n      with:\n        file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\n        s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n    - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2156\n        find . -iname \"core.[1-9]*\" -exec docker exec \"${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}\" sh -c \"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\" \\;\n\n    - name: Store Core dumps on S3\n      uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5\n      if: failure()\n      with:\n        name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\n        retention-days: 14\n        if-no-files-found: ignore\n        path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\n\n    - name: Teardown Linux\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main\n      if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\n\n    # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with\n    # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does\n    # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:\n    #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\n    #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\n    #     unknown error\n    #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\n    #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\n    #\n    # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\n    # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\n    - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\n      if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome != 'skipped'\n      shell: bash\n      env:\n        RUNNER_WORKSPACE: ${{ runner.workspace }}\n      run: |\n        set +e\n        set -x\n\n        nvidia-smi\n        # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\n        # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\n        # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\n        # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n        # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\n        # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\n        # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\n        # |===============================+======================+======================|\n        # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n        # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n        # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n        # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | Processes:                                                                  |\n        # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n        # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n        # |=============================================================================|\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        #\n        # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n        # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n        #\n        # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n        # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n        nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n        # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n        GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n        if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n","numLines":410,"startLine":1,"totalLines":410}}}

Here are some symptoms:\n    #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\n    #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\n    #     unknown error\n    #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\n    #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\n    #\n    # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\n    # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\n    - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\n      if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome != 'skipped'\n      shell: bash\n      env:\n        RUNNER_WORKSPACE: ${{ runner.workspace }}\n      run: |\n        set +e\n        set -x\n\n        nvidia-smi\n        # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\n        # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\n        # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\n        # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n        # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\n        # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\n        # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\n        # |===============================+======================+======================|\n        # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n        # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n        # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n        # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | Processes:                                                                  |\n        # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n        # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n        # |=============================================================================|\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        #\n        # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n        # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n        #\n        # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n        # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n        nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n        # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n        GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n        if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. GPU-related issue on G5 runners with\n    # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does\n    # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:\n    #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\n    #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\n    #     unknown error\n    #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\n    #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\n    #\n    # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\n    # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\n    - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\n      if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome != 'skipped'\n      shell: bash\n      env:\n        RUNNER_WORKSPACE: ${{ runner.workspace }}\n      run: |\n        set +e\n        set -x\n\n        nvidia-smi\n        # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\n        # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\n        # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\n        # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n        # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\n        # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\n        # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\n        # |===============================+======================+======================|\n        # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n        # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n        # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n        # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | Processes:                                                                  |\n        # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n        # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n        # |=============================================================================|\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        #\n        # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n        # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n        #\n        # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n        # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n        nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n        # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n        GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n        if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":88,"oldLines":7,"newStart":88,"newLines":7,"lines":["         echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\""," ","     - name: Pull docker image","-      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main","+      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10","       with:","         docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main\n      if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\n\n    # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with\n    # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does\n    # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:\n    #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\n    #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\n    #     unknown error\n    #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\n    #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\n    #\n    # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\n    # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\n    - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\n      if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome != 'skipped'\n      shell: bash\n      env:\n        RUNNER_WORKSPACE: ${{ runner.workspace }}\n      run: |\n        set +e\n        set -x\n\n        nvidia-smi\n        # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\n        # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\n        # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\n        # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n        # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\n        # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\n        # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\n        # |===============================+======================+======================|\n        # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n        # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n        # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n        # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | Processes:                                                                  |\n        # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n        # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n        # |=============================================================================|\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        #\n        # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n        # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n        #\n        # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n        # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n        nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n        # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n        GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n        if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":99,"oldLines":7,"newStart":99,"newLines":7,"lines":[" ","     - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG","       id: install-nvidia-driver","-      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main","+      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10","       if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}"," ","     - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

However, the rest of the information\n        # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n        # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n        # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n        # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n        # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | Processes:                                                                  |\n        # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n        # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n        # |=============================================================================|\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        #\n        # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n        # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n        #\n        # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n        # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n        nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n        # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n        GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n        if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":328,"oldLines":7,"newStart":328,"newLines":7,"lines":["         path: ./**/core.[1-9]*"," ","     - name: Teardown Linux","-      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main","+      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10","       if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'"," ","     # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
